title: W'Kabi
content:
{{CharHub|W'Kabi|W'Kabi}} {{Character |image = Black Panther Textless Character Poster 05.jpg |real name = W'Kabi |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|WKD}} |gender = Male |title = Royal Advisor (formerly)
Head of Security (formerly) |affiliation = [[Border Tribe]]
[[Tribal Council]] (formerly) |movie = {{Infobox List|''[[Black Panther (film)|Black Panther]]''
{{Collapse|''[[Black Panther: Wakanda Forever]]'' (mentioned)}}}} |book = ''[[Black Panther: A Novel]]'' |actor = [[Daniel Kaluuya]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|You let the refugees in, you let in all their problems. And then [[Wakanda]] is like everywhere else. Now if you said you wanted me and my [[Border Tribe|men]] to go out there and clean up the [[Earth|world]], then I'll be all for it.|W'Kabi to [[T'Challa]]|Black Panther (film)}} '''W'Kabi''' is the former head of security for [[Wakanda]]'s [[Border Tribe]] and the husband of [[Okoye]]. Having lost his faith in both [[T'Chaka]] and T'Challa due to their failures to capture [[Ulysses Klaue]], the terrorist responsible for his parents' deaths, W'Kabi lent his support to [[Erik Killmonger]] after he had killed Klaue, and supported the outsider in his quest to ascend to the throne of Wakanda. When T'Challa returned to challenge Killmonger once more, W'Kabi chose to continue aiding Killmonger and led the fight against T'Challa and [[M'Baku]], leading to him being forced by Okoye to surrender. He was then banished from Wakandan society, although he continued to remain in the nation. ==Biography== ===Early Life=== W'Kabi was born in [[Wakanda]], a technologically advanced and isolationist country, living with his parents and the [[Border Tribe]]. During his childhood, he met [[T'Challa]] with whom he maintained a strong friendly relationship.{{Ref|BP}} ====[[Attack on Wakanda]]==== {{Quote|My parents were killed when [[Ulysses Klaue|he]] [[Attack on Wakanda|attacked]]. Not a day goes by when I do not think about what Klaue took from us. From me.|W'Kabi to [[T'Challa|Black Panther]]|Black Panther (film)}} One day, as Wakanda prospered, a weapons trafficker named [[Ulysses Klaue]] managed to infiltrate Wakanda and steal a quarter-ton of [[vibranium]]. To facilitate his escape, Klaue triggered a bomb at the border, killing several members of the Border Tribe, including W'Kabi's parents. Unable to recover from this trauma, he began to devote a deep hatred to Klaue and swore to hunt him down until his death. Years later, W'Kabi became the leader of the Border Tribe, which offered him a place on the [[Tribal Council]] with the king and father of his best friend, [[T'Chaka]]. He also met [[Okoye]] and the two fell in love with each other. Throughout T'Chaka's reign, W'Kabi was disappointed by his King's supposed failure to find Klaue and was therefore desperately awaiting the arrival of a new King.{{Ref|BP}} ===New King=== ====[[Coronation of T'Challa|T'Challa's Coronation]]==== [[File:Border Tribe.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi stands alongside the [[Border Tribe]]]] {{Quote|[[T'Challa]]!|W'Kabi|Black Panther (film)}} Following the death of king [[T'Chaka]] in [[Vienna]], W'Kabi's friend [[T'Challa]] returned to [[Wakanda]] to be crowned and become the new king. A big ceremony was organized in his honor, and all of Wakanda gathered to dance and sing before the ritual fight. After the festivities, all the tribes gathered at the [[Warrior Falls]] to assist the fight. The shaman [[Zuri]] then welcomed T'Challa and asked all the tribes if one of their warriors had the will to fight for the throne. W'Kabi then began to refuse that a member of his tribe confront T'Challa. [[File:W'Kabi T'Challa-M'Baku Duel.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi roots for [[T'Challa]] to win the duel]] While no tribe accepted the challenge, the [[Jabari Tribe]] intervened spectacularly while shouting although they were unexpected. The chief of this tribe, [[M'Baku]], agreed to challenge T'Challa, considering him as incapable of governing as his father. The challenge took place and, after heavy blows, T'Challa was largely dominated by his opponent. W'Kabi then supported his friend by encouraging him with every stroke he took. T'Challa ended up winning the fight while sparing M'Baku.{{Ref|BP}} ====Learning Klaue's Location==== [[File:8GooZ17 d.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi offers some advice for [[T'Challa]]]] {{Quote|Take me with you. We'll take [[Ulysses Klaue|him]] down together side by side."
"I need you [[Wakanda|here]]. Protecting the border."
"Then I ask... you kill him where he stands, or you bring him back to us."
"You have my word. I will bring him back.|W'Kabi and [[T'Challa]]|Black Panther (film)}} Following the [[Coronation of T'Challa]], W'Kabi went to his rhinoceros pen to feed his animals. [[T'Challa]] joined him and complimented him on the fact that his rhinos had grown up under the wing of his friend. W'Kabi replied that he had learned of [[Nakia]]'s return to [[Wakanda]] and asked his friend if their relationship would resume. T'Challa, embarrassed, replied that Nakia wanted to improve Wakanda's international relations by letting impotent refugees into the country, which ran counter to W'Kabi's traditions and advice. [[File:WKabiAsksTChallaIfHeCanKillUKlaue.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi gives his input to the [[Tribal Council]]]] The latter replied that allowing refugees to enter would bring problems with them, which would lead to Wakanda becoming a country like the others. However, W'Kabi proposed to T'Challa to let him take his army to restore order and peace in the world, which he would do without hesitation. T'Challa, attached to the traditions of the country, was perplexed when using violence.{{Ref|BP}} ====Losing Faith==== [[File:W'KabiFurious.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi asks [[T'Challa]] about his [[Arrest of Ulysses Klaue|mission]]]] {{Quote|For thirty years, your [[T'Chaka|father]] was in power and did nothing. With you, I thought it'd be different. But it's more of the same.|W'Kabi to [[T'Challa]]|Black Panther (film)}} Hearing the return of [[T'Challa]], [[Okoye]], and [[Nakia]], W'Kabi rushed to [[Shuri's Lab]] to see if his friend had brought [[Ulysses Klaue]] back as he had promised. He found the king at the entrance to the lab and asked if he brought Klaue back, looking all around him with a lot of enthusiasm. T'Challa told him that Klaue escaped just after being captured. [[File:W'KabiFurious2.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi is furious with [[T'Challa]]'s failure]] W'Kabi changed his expression directly and explained to T'Challa that for many years he lost hope in [[T'Chaka]], but when his son was crowned, he had hopes things were going to change. W'Kabi ended up saying that he had nothing more to support for T'Challa, that was to say that the way of acting did not yet have a difference compared to that of the former king. After a last look of disappointment, W'Kabi left the room and returned to the border.{{Ref|BP}} ====Meeting Killmonger==== [[File:Border Tribe Killmonger's Arrival.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi is approached by [[Erik Killmonger]]]] {{Quote|What is [[Ulysses Klaue|this]]?"
"Just a little gift.|W'Kabi and [[Erik Killmonger]]|Black Panther (film)}} While W'Kabi was monitoring the [[Wakanda]] border, he saw a man, [[Erik Killmonger]], get off a plane and arrive with a long bag. W'Kabi approached him with his men and asked him what the bag contained. Killmonger told him that it was a present and started to open the bag, which did not please W'Kabi and his men who drew their weapons. Killmonger hardly paid attention to it and still opened the bag under the petrified eyes of W'Kabi who discovered [[Ulysses Klaue]]'s corpse. W'Kabi then asked Killmonger who he was right before reporting the [[Tribal Council]]. By taking Killmonger, he discovered that he is wearing the same [[Wakandan Royal Ring|royal ring]] as T'Challa. [[File:BP Teaser Trailer 43.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi escorts [[Erik Killmonger]] into the throne room]] Once the Tribal Council met, W'Kabi opened the door and accompanied Killmonger so that he could explain. Killmonger openly exposed his plan to conquer the throne in order to restore order in the world, which provoked a laugh from the Ancients. [[T'Challa]] then went to Killmonger telling him that he knew who he was. Killmonger then provoked the whole room by asking them to ask who he was, [[Shuri]] replied that his name is [[Erik Stevens]], and that he was a former [[United States Navy]] soldier, to which Killmonger responded that this was absolutely not his true identity. [[File:Tumblr p21qoi92iO1w8xetso1 1280.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi witnesses [[Erik Killmonger]]'s testimony]] T'Challa refused to ask him his name and continued to tell his guards to take him away, and ordered W'Kabi and his men to remove him from the room. The [[River Tribe Elder]] could not help asking for the identity of the stranger, and Killmonger revealed that his name was actually [[N'Jadaka]], son of Prince [[N'Jobu]] and legitimate heir to the throne. The whole room being in shock, [[Ramonda]] suspected him of lying, but W'Kabi proved the opposite by showing the royal ring. Killmonger therefore proposed to T'Challa to [[Coronation of Erik Killmonger|challenge him]] to give him a chance to become the new King of Wakanda, believing that he was legitimate.{{Ref|BP}} ====[[Coronation of Erik Killmonger|Defeat of T'Challa]]==== As W'Kabi watched on, [[T'Challa]] therefore accepted [[Erik Killmonger]]'s request and the two met on [[Warrior Falls]] to fight for the throne. During the fight, T'Challa was largely dominated by Killmonger, who during the fight explained his motives for governing [[Wakanda]] and offering help to the most oppressed people. His speech appealed to W'Kabi who, despite the fact that T'Challa was his best friend, supported Killmonger to finally hope to see Wakanda accept his revolutionary ideas. [[File:WKabiSeeingTChallaDefeated-BP.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi witness [[Erik Killmonger]]'s coronation]] When Killmonger prepared to deal the fatal blow to his opponent, [[Zuri]] stopped him by saying that he is the only person to blame for the [[Murder of N'Jobu|murder]] of his [[N'Jobu|father]]. In response, Killmonger killed Zuri in front of the public, and T'Challa screamed in pain at the death of his friend. In the next second, Killmonger finished T'Challa and threw him into the river before gloating of his tyranny as the King. Having won the fight, Killmonger claimed to be the new king.{{Ref|BP}} ====Following Killmonger's Rule==== [[File:BP W'Kabi Listens to Killmonger.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi listens to [[Erik Killmonger]]'s speech]] {{Quote|The world is changing, [[Okoye|General]]. [[Tribal Council|Elders]], it is getting smaller. The outside world is catching up, and soon it will be the conquerors or the conquered. I'd rather be the former.|W'Kabi|Black Panther (film)}} Satisfied with [[Erik Killmonger]]'s victory, W'Kabi waited for his arrival at the [[Tribal Council]] with [[Okoye]], the [[Dora Milaje]] and the Ancients. Killmonger then went to his throne and sat down, expressing a sense of accomplishment. He then explained his plan to help oppressed countries around the world by offering [[Vibranium]] weapons to [[War Dogs]] all around the globe. [[File:BP Conquerors or the Conquered.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi expresses his opinion to the [[Tribal Council|council]]]] W'Kabi agreed until Okoye said that Killmonger's methods are not the ones [[Wakanda]] generally used. W'Kabi replied that the world is changing, and that Wakanda should do the same to stay better and not be conquered, which did not please Okoye, who abstained from answering. Killmonger ultimately ordered him to respect the orders he was given and begin the operation.{{Ref|BP}} ====Confronted by Okoye==== [[File:Okoye & W'Kabi Arguement 1.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi and [[Okoye]] argue about their operation]] {{Quote|[[Ulysses Klaue|Klaue]] [[Attack on Wakanda|stole]] from us! He murdered my mother and father! Thirty years there's been no justice, and [[T'Challa]] swore to bring him back! Not take him to the [[United Nations|UN]], not take him to the [[CIA]], but bring him here to [[Wakanda|us]]!|W'Kabi to [[Okoye]]|Black Panther (film)}} Away from prying eyes, [[Okoye]] then had words with her husband, perturbed by his support of [[Erik Killmonger]]'s usurpation of the throne. W'Kabi, however, found every justification he could, as he remembered losing his parents to [[Ulysses Klaue]]'s incursion into [[Wakanda]] to steal their sacred [[vibranium]]. Despite Okoye's defense of [[T'Challa]]'s limited courses of actions for handling the situation, W'Kabi's anger and frustration was visible. [[File:Okoye & W'Kabi Arguement 3.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi loses his faith in [[T'Challa]]]] As they continued to debate the idea, W'Kabi voiced support for Killmonger's plan to conquer the world under a united Wakandan rule and to have their children live as rulers, something which greatly disturbed a traditionalist such as Okoye, who also saw the logic in Killmonger's ideology as no different than colonizers of past eras in human history. Following their heated talk, the two parted ways, though bitterly, to return to their regular duties. W'Kabi felt as though he did not reach his wife, while Okoye felt her husband had been easily radicalized by Killmonger.{{Ref|BPDS}} ====[[Battle of Mount Bashenga]]==== [[File:BP Killmonger (Orders Weapons).png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi gives his orders out to [[Erik Killmonger]]]] {{Quote|You would kill me, my love?"
"For [[Wakanda]]? Without question.|W'Kabi and [[Okoye]]|Black Panther (film)}} On [[Mount Bashenga]], W'Kabi stood beside [[Erik Killmonger]] and the [[Wakanda]]n armies as they prepared to begin the operation. Killmonger sent cargo planes across the border while W'Kabi followed him listening to his plan. Killmonger, ambitious, observed all the weapons in [[Vibranium]] but was interrupted by a big explosion of one of the planes he had launched. This explosion drew all the attention, and the [[T'Challa|Black Panther]] revealed himself, revealing to everyone that he was still alive and that, consequently, the fight was not over. [[File:GDUFJgR d.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi witnesses [[T'Challa]]'s sudden return]] Killmonger refused to accept the fact that the [[Coronation of Erik Killmonger|ritual fight]] was still in progress, considering that this ritual was now a thing of the past, and ordered W'Kabi to kill him. [[Okoye]] tried desperately to dissuade W'Kabi from obeying Killmonger, but W'Kabi, stubborn with the idea of ​​seeing [[Wakanda]] dominate the world, called his [[Border Tribe|tribe]] into battle against the Black Panther. W'Kabi is therefore right in front of his former friend and began to hit him with his vibranium billhook, but he was soon beaten. [[File:Black Panther (film) 120.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi commands the [[Border Tribe]] to attack]] [[T'Challa]] then urged him to surrender and stop the slaughter, but W'Kabi did not listen to a word and stood on a rock to call his [[White Rhinoceros]] to bombard their way into the battle. Rhinoceroses arrive on the battlefield and W'Kabi rode one to impinge on his path. With the help of his tribe, W'Kabi managed to encircle [[Nakia]], Okoye, [[Shuri]] and [[Ayo]] with energy shields. W'Kabi ordered them to go in exactly three seconds. [[File:W'KabiConfrontedByOkoye.png|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi is confronted by [[Okoye]]]] As he began his countdown, W'Kabi is violently interrupted by [[M'Baku]] and his [[Jabari Tribe]], that came to help the Black Panther in his fight. The energy shield was destroyed by the Jabari Tribe and the fight resumed. W'Kabi, determined to finish, used his rhinoceros to attack M'Baku, but his beast recognized Okoye who stood right in front of him, and started to lick her. [[File:BP - W'Kabi and Tribe Yields.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|W'Kabi is finally defeated by [[Okoye]]]] W'Kabi descended from his rhinoceros and Okoye demanded he lay down his weapon. Feeling betrayed, W'Kabi asked his wife if she would kill him. Okoye told him that she would do so without question for Wakanda and this finally caused him to realize just how twisted he had become in his aggression. Not wanting to see his people kill each other, W'Kabi surrendered and knelt down before his love, with all his tribe who followed him without hesitation.{{Ref|BP}} ===Banishment=== {{Quote|[[Okoye|She]] raised a [[Vibranium Spear|spear]] against her own husband for [[Wakanda]]!"
"Where is her treacherous husband now, but in a place where she can visit if she wished?!|[[M'Kathu]] and [[Ramonda]]|Black Panther: Wakanda Forever}} As consequence for his actions, W'Kabi was banished from [[Birnin Zana]] and relocated to the outskirts of the country.[https://www.cbr.com/wkabi-daniel-kaluuya-absence-explained-black-panther-2-mcu/ Black Panther 2 Director Reveals the Fate of Daniel Kaluuya's W'Kabi]{{Ref|BPWF}} ==Abilities== *'''Expert Combatant''': W'Kabi displayed excellent fighting skills, having led the border tribe during the [[Battle of Mount Bashenga]] and even managed to land a hit on [[T'Challa]], although he was quickly overpowered. Before his betrayal to the throne, W'Kabi offered to help T'Challa "clean up the world." *'''Expert Tactician''': Feeling betrayed by [[T'Challa]]'s failure to capture [[Ulysses Klaue]], W'Kabi allied with [[Erik Killmonger]] and led the [[Border Tribe]] against the rest of [[Wakanda]] in order to carry out Killmonger's plan. However, his love for [[Okoye]] eventually led to his surrender. {{Quote|Kuthetha."
"Speak.|[[T'Challa]] and W'Kabi to [[Erik Killmonger]]|Black Panther (film)}} *'''Bilingualism''': W'Kabi is fluent in his native Xhosa, as well as English with a Wakandan accent. Unaware that [[Erik Killmonger]] could speak Xhosa himself, W'Kabi provided translation for him when meeting with [[T'Challa]]. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''Vibranium Mambele''': A vibranium sickle-sword that W'Kabi used against T'Challa. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Kimoyo Beads]]''': A Wakandan device that allows him to communicate with others. *'''Blowhorn''': W'Kabi can summon his [[White Rhinoceros|white rhinoceros]] herd with the blow of his horn. ==Facilities== *'''[[Citadel]]''': When he was a member of the [[Tribal Council]], W'Kabi frequently attended meetings in the Citadel. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *Father † *Mother † *[[Korabo]] - Father-in-Law *Mother-in-Law *[[Okoye]] - Wife *[[M'Kathu]] - Uncle-by-Marriage *Children ===Allies=== *[[T'Chaka]] † - Former King *[[Zuri]] † *[[Tribal Council]] **[[River Tribe Elder]] **[[Merchant Tribe Elder]] **[[Zawavari]] *[[Wakandan Royal Guard]] *[[M20]] - Steed *[[Erik Killmonger|N'Jadaka/Erik Killmonger]] † - Former King {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Ulysses Klaue]] † *[[Golden Tribe]] - Former Allies **[[Ramonda]] † - Former Queen **[[T'Challa|T'Challa/Black Panther]] † - Former King and Former Friend **[[Shuri]] - Princess *[[Nakia]] - Former Ally *[[Dora Milaje]] - Former Allies **[[Ayo]] **[[Xoliswa]] † **[[Nomble]] **[[Yama]] *[[Jabari Tribe]] **[[M'Baku]] - Attempted Victim {{Col-end}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, W'Kabi was the chief of the [[Wakanda]]n security and [[T'Challa]]'s second-in-command. ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Daniel Kaluuya]] originally expressed interest in reprising his role as W'Kabi in ''[[Black Panther: Wakanda Forever]]'' as long as he liked the story despite being unaware if he would return or not.[https://screenrant.com/black-panther-2-daniel-kaluuya-wkabi-return-unsure/ Daniel Kaluuya Isn’t Sure If W’Kabi Will Return In Black Panther 2] Unfortunately, scheduling conflicts with ''{{WPS|Nope (film)|Nope}}'' prevented Kaluuya from returning as W'Kabi.[https://deadline.com/2022/07/daniel-kaluuya-wont-reprise-role-as-wkabi-in-black-panther-wakanda-forever-1235063584/ Daniel Kaluuya Won’t Reprise Role As W’Kabi In ‘Black Panther: Wakanda Forever’] *[[Brandon M. Shaw]] and [[Tomar Boyd]] were stunt doubles for [[Daniel Kaluuya]] in the role of W'Kabi. ==References== {{Gallery|W'Kabi/Gallery}} {{Wq|W'Kabi}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Black Panther (film) Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Black Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:Tribal Leaders]] [[Category:Criminals]] [[Category:Border Tribe Members]] [[Category:Tribal Council Members]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]]
